Love · Breathe · Climb

THE SCIENCE
OF BREATH
FOR CLIMBING

Pranaclimb bridges ancient pranayama with modern sports science — giving climbers a measurable edge in endurance, recovery, and focus on the wall.

Cornwall 2006

Pranaclimb

Methodology

A breath-led climbing performance system. Field-based, non-invasive, and built for climbing’s real-world demands — dynamic movement, anaerobic bursts, expressive breathing, and micro-rests.

Climbing performance is limited as much by breathing as by strength. The Pranaclimb Methodology combines breathing physiology, breathwork training, and field-based performance metrics to help climbers improve endurance, pacing, and recovery on the wall.

📄 Published Research: Anderson, A. (2025). The Pranaclimb Methodology: Non-Invasive Tracking of Critical Power and W′bal Modelling in Rock Climbing. SportRxiv. SportRxiv · ResearchGate

Methodology Q&A →

Self-Guided Diagnostic & Training Tool for Climbers

Remote Breathing Assessment

Your journey begins with a comprehensive remote assessment — on and off the rock.

The Pranaclimb RBA is a remote, self-administered or coached assessment that uses Breathing Rate (BR), Rate of Perceived Exertion (RPE), and Heart Rate Recovery (HRR₆₀) to identify performance zones, recovery capacity, and breathing efficiency in climbing.

  • Breathing Rate (BR)
  • RPE (CR-10) — perceived effort
  • Expressive breathing (holds, grunts, screams)
  • HRR₆₀ — heart rate recovery at 60 seconds
  • Deep Dive: CO₂ tolerance, PEFR, SpO₂, rib mobility

What's measured in the real world

The Book

A science-based guide to breathwork, endurance, and mindful performance. Learn how breathing transforms climbing — from the science of W′bal to the flow states of elite climbers.

From the physiology of CO₂ tolerance to breath cues for the crux, this book gives you everything you need to make breathing your greatest competitive advantage.

    Built with Kit

    No spam. Just breathing science, climbing insights, and early access to the book.

    Annie Anderson

    Founder of the Pranaclimb Methodology

    Independent Researcher · Breathwork Specialist · Cornwall

    Annie Anderson is an independent researcher, breathwork specialist, and lifelong climber with over 30 years of worldwide experience. Creator of the Pranaclimb Methodology — a field-adapted system using BR, RPE and HRR₆₀ to track performance thresholds in real-time. Published on SportRxiv and ResearchGate.

    Based on the sea cliffs of Cornwall. Her philosophy — live, love life every day — carried her through three years of Stage 4 Renal Cell Carcinoma and surgery.

    Meet Annie →

    Sister Brand

    OceanDips

    Cold water · Cornwall · Breathwork

    Annie swims in the icy Atlantic every day — not just for the cold, but for the breath. Cold water and breathwork are deeply connected: the cold forces you to regulate your nervous system through breath control, building resilience, mental clarity, and physiological strength.

    Ocean Dips is Annie’s sister brand — guiding people into cold water safely, mindfully, and powerfully. Based on the sea cliffs of Cornwall, it bridges the gap between cold water swimming, breathwork, and performance.

    🌊 Visit Ocean Dips oceandips.com
